events.title: From Burnout to Balance

coverImage.alt: From Burnout to Balance event cover

events.shortDescription: An event exploring strategies for managing burnout and finding balance in your career and life.

events.title: She Sharp & academyEX: International Women's Day 2026

coverImage.alt: She Sharp & academyEX International Women's Day 2026 - Together We Can Forge Gender Equality Collectively

events.shortDescription: Join She Sharp and academyEX for an inspiring International Women's Day celebration centred on this year's theme — Give To Gain. An evening of honest conversations, shared experiences, and meaningful connections.

detailPageData.title: She Sharp & academyEX: International Women's Day 2026
detailPageData.subtitle: Celebrating women. Inspiring action. Driving equity.
fullDescription: She Sharp is partnering again with academyEX for International Women's Day 2026, creating space for our community to connect, reflect, and take meaningful action toward gender equality.

This year's IWD theme, Give To Gain, is a reminder that progress grows through community. When we give our time, support, knowledge, and encouragement, we help open doors for women and girls — and everyone benefits.

Join us for a welcoming evening focused on honest conversations, shared experiences, and building connections. This event is about showing up for one another and taking small but meaningful steps together.
Tickets are available at three tiers: Donation/Koha (free), Students ($15 NZD), and Professionals ($20 NZD). No refunds.

speakers.name: Ana Ivanovic-Tongue
speakers.title: Chief Delivery Officer
speakers.bio: Ana Ivanovic-Tongue leads large-scale learning programmes at academyEX, partnering with public and private organisations across New Zealand and Australia. She blends deep programme leadership expertise with a passion for making innovation in learning practical, scalable and human. A member of New Zealand's AI Forum Executive Council, Ana contributes to national conversations on AI adoption and innovation. With 25 years' experience across arts, culture, government, digital transformation and EdTech, plus a Master of Technological Futures focused on AI and art crime, she helps adults build confidence, creativity and resilience in the age of disruption.

speakers.name: Danubi Paim (Dani)
speakers.title: Founder, Property Besties
speakers.bio: Dani Paim bought her first property at 19 with no safety net — just determination and fear. Today she's the founder of Property Besties, New Zealand's most collaborative property investor community (100+ members), and Wealth & Wellness Co., helping women build financial independence without sacrificing what matters most. A former financial advisor, Dani saw women needed clarity, confidence and community — not more information. She left the 9–5 to create supportive, values-driven spaces. She's coached 200+ Kiwis and is on a mission to help 1,000 women invest in property while living fully now, not just at retirement.

speakers.name: Wyndi Tagi
speakers.title: Co-Founder WE Mana & The Table
speakers.bio: Wyndi Tagi is a business strategist, speaker, and community builder passionate about helping people live with purpose and lead with mana. After building and successfully exiting her accounting firm, she now supports purpose-driven founders to grow sustainable businesses without losing themselves in the process. As founder of The Table, a global community for culturally aligned business owners, Wyndi creates spaces where women belong, build, and become. She believes in the power of "Give To Gain" — that when women share wisdom, courage, and support, everyone rises.

speakers.name: Annette Rangi
speakers.title: GM - Digital Transformation
speakers.bio: Annette Rangi is GM of Digital Transformation at HEB Construction, bringing extensive leadership experience across IT, digital delivery, and business transformation. With a career spanning roles as CTO, GM Operations, and Programme Manager across sectors including construction, retail, energy, and government, she champions agile leadership, innovation, and emerging technology adoption. A passionate advocate for diversity, Annette has coached and mentored teams of over 150 people and is experienced in Scaled/Lean-Agile, SAFe, SCRUM, and DevOps methodologies.

speakers.name: Camille Elemia
speakers.title: Journalist turned Product Manager, NZME
speakers.bio: Camille Elemia is an award-winning international journalist turned digital product manager at NZME, where she helps shape the future of digital news experiences at the New Zealand Herald. Over 15 years she reported for The New York Times, BBC, Bloomberg, and Al Jazeera, turning complex global issues into compelling stories. A Fulbright fellow and author of the book Unrequited Love: Duterte's China Embrace, Camille is currently completing a Master of Technological Futures at academyEX, exploring emerging disruptive technologies and their social impact.

events.title: She Sharp & Candice Murray: Own your Energy!

coverImage.alt: She Sharp & Candice Murray: Own your Energy! workshop banner

events.shortDescription: An interactive career mindset workshop with coach Candice Murray, focused on helping you consciously shift your energy so you can show up with more calm, confidence, and self-belief in the moments that matter.

detailPageData.title: She Sharp & Candice Murray: Own your Energy!
detailPageData.subtitle: Career Mindset Workshop
fullDescription: How you feel directly impacts how you show up at work and in life. In this engaging and practical workshop, Career Mindset Coach Candice Murray will guide you through how to consciously choose and access the emotional state that would be most helpful right now — whether that is calm, confidence, self-belief, or happiness.
You will learn powerful, easy-to-use tools that help you shift your energy on demand, so you can perform at your best in the moments that matter, from important conversations to everyday challenges.

The session is designed to be interactive, supportive, and fun, creating a safe space to reflect, participate, and experiment with new ways of thinking and responding. You will leave feeling energised and equipped with practical tools you can immediately apply in both your career and personal life.
The event flow includes time for registration and networking, a 90-minute workshop, and post-workshop networking to connect with other attendees.

speakers.name: Candice Murray
speakers.title: Career Mindset Coach
speakers.bio: Candice Murray is a Career Mindset Coach recognised as one of Influence Digest Media's Top Coaches in Auckland for 2024 and 2025. She has delivered workshops for organisations including AWS, Spark New Zealand, and AUT University, helping professionals build confidence, clarity, and momentum. Drawing on her own background in tech, she is known for her warm, relatable facilitation style and her ability to translate mindset concepts into practical, real-world tools.

events.title: HER WAKA — Navigating Pathways into Sustainable Employment

coverImage.alt: HER WAKA — Navigating pathways into sustainable employment, delivered by SheSharp in Auckland

events.shortDescription: A dynamic, employment-focused programme delivered by SheSharp for MSD clients ready to strengthen their pathway into work. March session theme: AI & The Future of Work.

detailPageData.title: HER WAKA — Navigating Pathways into Sustainable Employment
detailPageData.subtitle: AI & The Future of Work — March 2026
fullDescription: HER WAKA is a dynamic, employment-focused programme delivered by SheSharp in Auckland for Ministry of Social Development clients who are ready to strengthen their pathway into work. Running monthly from March to June 2026, each session is a cohesive, repeatable event designed to build job readiness, confidence, and practical understanding of the New Zealand labour market.

Each two-hour programme brings together up to 25 participants in a supportive, high-impact environment that blends inspiration, practical skills, and direct employer insight. Through real conversations and structured learning, participants gain clarity on how to position themselves confidently and competitively in today's job market.

Our March HER WAKA session focuses on Artificial Intelligence and the evolving tech job market in 2026. Participants can look forward to a dynamic AI Panel Discussion featuring industry professionals, a short hands-on workshop exploring practical AI tools and how they are shaping workplaces, and a special guest speaker from RCSA sharing insights into the 2026 tech hiring landscape and what employers are looking for.
This session will help participants understand how AI is influencing job opportunities, recruitment trends, and the skills that matter most right now.
HER WAKA is more than an event — it is a practical, supportive step toward sustainable employment and long-term workforce participation.

speakers.name: Nikita Kumari
speakers.title: PMP-certified Product and Project Manager
speakers.bio: Nikita Kumari is a PMP-certified Product and Project Manager with over 6 years of experience delivering AI and digital products in the B2B SaaS space. She began her career as a Python developer, building a strong technical foundation before moving into product and agile delivery roles. Her work includes AI and machine learning initiatives focused on improving decision-making, automating workflows, and enhancing user experience. Now based in Auckland, she shares insights for professionals building careers in product, tech, and AI.

speakers.name: Chan Meng
speakers.title: Senior AI/ML Infrastructure and Full-Stack Engineer
speakers.bio: Chan Meng is a Senior AI/ML Infrastructure and Full-Stack Engineer specialising in generative AI, autonomous agentic systems, and scalable cloud-native applications. She is a Founding Engineer at Gavigo and CTO at Sanicle. Her work includes advanced RAG architectures, LLM orchestration, and microservices. With a Master's in Applied Computing and a background in mathematics education, Chan combines strong technical expertise with leadership, speaking globally at events including UN CSW69 and mentoring women in STEM.

speakers.name: Meeta Patel
speakers.title: Programme Lead and Lead Facilitator, Leading Change for Good
speakers.bio: Dr Patel is Programme Lead and Lead Facilitator for Leading Change for Good at AcademyEX, with a career spanning industry, research, and academia. With a background as a research scientist, she spent many years in applied research before moving into education. Outside of professional work, she is an Ambassador for She Sharp, driven by a strong belief in equity and the power of people to create change.

speakers.name: Abe Naus
speakers.title: General Manager
speakers.bio: Abe Naus is General Manager of Potentia. He is an experienced IT, Projects and Change Recruiter who has been working in recruitment for the past 16 years. He's built a strong reputation in the local market, priding himself on providing a premium service for both clients and candidates. His recruitment team's areas of expertise include Project Services, DevOps, Big Data & Analytics, Architects, Developers, Test Analysts, Business Analysts, AI, and Agile skilled individuals.

speakers.name: Paul Kelly
speakers.title: Business Manager and Principal Consultant
speakers.bio: Paul Kelly is a Business Manager and Principal Consultant at Absolute IT, bringing extensive experience in the technology recruitment sector across New Zealand.

speakers.name: Neekee Reshamwala
speakers.title: Business Manager for Auckland
speakers.bio: Neekee Reshamwala brings over a decade of experience in recruitment, particularly within the banking, finance, insurance, and government industries. She is passionate about connecting top talent with leading organisations. Before joining Randstad Digital, Neekee held senior leadership roles in the recruitment industry. She holds a Master's degree in Business Management from Massey University and has an in-depth understanding of Auckland's job market.

specialSections.title: What to Expect
content: Inspirational speakers sharing authentic employment journeys and practical lessons from navigating the New Zealand workforce
Employer and recruiter panels offering direct insight into current hiring expectations
Practical job-readiness sessions focused on professional presentation, interview confidence, and effective employer engagement
Clear and accessible employment rights guidance
Structured networking opportunities with employers, recruiters, and industry representatives

events.title: Her Waka (April 2026) — #IAmRemarkable & Vibe Coding

coverImage.alt: Her Waka April 2026 — #IAmRemarkable & Vibe Coding, delivered by SheSharp in Auckland

events.shortDescription: The second HER WAKA cohort focuses on building confidence through the #IAmRemarkable workshop and exploring Vibe Coding — using free AI tools to build a personal website through natural conversation.

detailPageData.title: Her Waka (April 2026) — #IAmRemarkable & Vibe Coding
detailPageData.subtitle: #IAmRemarkable & Vibe Coding
fullDescription: The second cohort of the HER WAKA series continues with a session focused on building confidence, celebrating achievements, and exploring practical AI tools.

We will begin with #IAmRemarkable, a global movement that encourages everyone, especially those from underrepresented groups, to celebrate their achievements and challenge the stigma around self-promotion. In this engaging 50-minute workshop, She Sharp Founder and Director Dr Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh) will guide participants through practical exercises to help recognise achievements, own your story, and confidently share your impact.

We will also introduce Vibe Coding, showing how anyone, even without a technical background, can use free AI tools to build and launch their own personal website through simple, natural conversations with AI.
As with the first cohort, RCSA will also be joining this session to share valuable insights on the current job market and support participants with practical job-seeking guidance.

HER WAKA is a dynamic, employment-focused programme delivered by SheSharp in Auckland for Ministry of Social Development clients who are ready to strengthen their pathway into work. Running monthly from March to June 2026, each session is a cohesive, repeatable event designed to build job readiness, confidence, and practical understanding of the New Zealand labour market.

speakers.name: Dr. Mahsa McCauley (Mohaghegh)
speakers.title: Associate Professor at AUT and Chair AI Forum
speakers.bio: Dr. Mahsa McCauley is Founder and Director of She Sharp Charitable Trust. As Chair of AI Forum NZ and Associate Professor of AI at AUT, she researches AI, machine learning, IoT and cybersecurity. She serves on boards of NZTech, EdTechNZ and World Summit Awards, advancing AI strategy, education and SDG projects. Appointed Communication and Information Commissioner for NZ National Commission for UNESCO in January 2026, she leads efforts to uphold freedom of expression, expand information access and foster ethical ecosystems.

speakers.name: Chan Meng
speakers.title: Senior AI/ML Infrastructure and Full-Stack Engineer
speakers.bio: Chan Meng is a Senior AI/ML Infrastructure and Full-Stack Engineer specialising in generative AI, autonomous agentic systems, and scalable cloud-native applications. She is a Founding Engineer at Gavigo and CTO at Sanicle, leading end-to-end technical delivery using Next.js, Node.js, Python, Docker, and Kubernetes. Her work includes advanced RAG architectures, LLM orchestration, and microservices. With a Master's in Applied Computing and a background in mathematics education, Chan combines strong technical expertise with leadership, speaking globally at events including UN CSW69 and mentoring women in STEM.
